appeal_letter:
Dear Cigna Appeals Reviewer,

I am appealing the denial of coverage for a Roux-en-Y Gastric Bypass requested on my behalf.

The denial states that, while my BMI of 41 meets the initial criteria for bariatric surgery, the request was denied as not medically necessary because the records show four months of documented dietary counseling rather than the six consecutive months of a medically supervised weight loss program required by Cigna's Medical Coverage Policy for Bariatric Surgery.

I want to address that specific criterion directly rather than around it. I acknowledge that the documentation reflects four months of supervised dietary counseling, not the full six months the policy cites. My position is that the individualized clinical facts in this case warrant an exception to a strict application of the six-month threshold. My BMI of 41 is accompanied by life-threatening sleep apnea and hypertension. My cardiologist has ordered that the surgery be expedited because the sleep apnea is causing severe right heart strain, and has identified a six-month delay as clinically dangerous in my circumstances. The purpose of a supervised weight loss period is to support an appropriate and durable surgical decision, and in this case the documented cardiac risk makes further delay a danger to my health rather than a benefit. Applied to these documented facts, the medical necessity standard supports approving the requested surgery now.

I also note that the denial advised I may appeal within 180 days, but it did not describe the process for submitting an appeal or confirm my right to a full and fair review of this decision. I am asserting my right to a complete and fair review, including consideration of the cardiologist's findings, and I ask that the appeal process and review rights be provided to me in writing.

I respectfully request that Cigna overturn the denial and approve coverage for the Roux-en-Y Gastric Bypass based on the individualized clinical circumstances described above, or provide a full and fair review that takes the documented cardiac risk into account.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Cardiologist's written statement detailing the severe right heart strain and why a six-month delay is clinically dangerous
Documentation of the sleep apnea diagnosis and severity (e.g., sleep study results)
Records confirming the hypertension diagnosis and current management
Documentation of the four months of medically supervised dietary counseling, with dates and supervising provider
Any record of the expedited surgery order from the cardiologist
A copy of the cited Cigna Medical Coverage Policy for Bariatric Surgery, including any exception provisions
Written confirmation of the appeal submission process and full-and-fair-review rights
